## [00:00 - 03:02]

So why don't people buy from you? Why does somebody hit your website, phone your company, drive all the way to your retail showroom and then not buy? Well one reason could be this and you need to know what they all are. Many times the customer won't tell you and or they don't even know. Number one, they're not qualified. They are not qualified to buy your product right now and will not tell you, can't tell you. Number two, they can't tell you why they won't buy right now. Maybe there's something going on personally that they just can't disclose to you. Number three, they're not a decision-maker. Maybe as we go through these you write them down, memorize them and start checking off during the day when they're happening. Number three, they're not a decision-maker. They are not the decision-maker. Look you could have combinations of these affect you. They're not a decision-maker and can't tell you they're not the decision-maker. I'm sure you've had that happen. Number four, they're not ready. They're telling you they're ready. They're telling you they're going to do something but they're not ready. I know people that bargain hunt constantly and they're going to tell you I'm ready to do this right now and the truth is they're not. They're just using that as a negotiating strategy to see what their deal would be if they were ready. Number five, they don't have enough information. There's not yet enough information for them to make a decision so they can't. Now they've become a follow-up. Don't lump them together with the first four or you're going to be missing strategies to follow up what people that don't have just enough information. Number six, just not prepared to buy. This could be a financial situation. Could be information. It could be I just don't have enough personal certainty within myself to buy. Maybe there's so much uncertainty going on in the world right now that emotionally I'm out of whack so much. I don't know. Maybe the stars in a moon aren't lined up. They're just not prepared to do it. Number seven, big reason okay on wrong product. Particularly when you have physical products and I wouldn't say just physical tangible or even intangible. You want to always check are we on the right product? Would you consider another product? Would you consider the one above this below this? Would you consider one if it was a little more money or a little less money? That way in that way we could find out that people would consider. If you'd consider a different piece of art then you're on the wrong one. Number eight, you don't know what they need. You have no clue. What problem are they trying to solve? What is it they're trying to replace? What is it they're trying to avoid? What situation are they trying to get a handle on to make this decision and buy from you? Don't know what they need if you don't know and they don't know. Nobody's doing anything. Number nine, look the timing could be wrong. It could just be the wrong timing. I've so I've I've gone to companies and done the best job had everybody excited. Everybody was on board and what I didn't know was the company was actually in a buy sale and the owner of the company couldn't tell me. The managers didn't know. Upper management couldn't say it.
